level_signals: 7+ years of progressive experience in Product Management
Demonstrated success defining, launching, and scaling complex products from ideation to general availability
Partner with CTO and engineering leads, suggesting senior-level scope

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of progressive experience in Product Management, ideally within an enterprise or B2B setting
must_haves.qualifier: ideally within an enterprise or B2B setting

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrated success in defining, launching, and scaling complex products from ideation to general availability in an enterprise environment
Proven ability to manage diverse stakeholders (Engineering, Design, Sales, Marketing, Executive Leadership) and drive cross-functional alignment on product vision and prioritization
Exceptional ability to map, analyze, and refine Customer User Journeys (CUJs) to identify high-value pain points and opportunities for AI solution deployment
Highly data-driven approach to prioritization and decision-making, including the ability to define KPIs, create product impact dashboards, and synthesize data into actionable product requirements
A track record of leveraging qualitative (customer interviews) and quantitative (product usage data) insights to validate assumptions and drive product strategy
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts and product strategies clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Hands-on experience developing and launching AI and ML-powered products or features for internal or external customers
Robust functional and technical understanding of LLMs and common AI-based workflows (e.g., retrieval-augmented generation, fine-tuning, prompt engineering)
Prior experience working directly with LLMs or launching conversational AI agents into production
Technical background (e.g., Computer Science degree or equivalent practical experience) that allows for deep engagement with engineering teams on technical trade-offs and architectural decisions

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working with cross-geo and cross-timezone environments
Track record of solving high-impact, cross-system challenges and aligning senior stakeholders across Product, Engineering, and Core infrastructure organizations

scope.surface: Infrastructure and Data Experiences (IDX) — Core Infra, Developer Infra, Data Platform, Service Platform, internal AI agents
primary_metrics_inferred: KPIs and product impact dashboards for developer products
cross_functional_partners: Engineering (Core Infra, Developer Infra, Data Platform, Service Platform)
core_responsibilities: Partner with engineering teams to ship functionality that delights users and makes a measurable impact on the business
Establish system and design principles for how we build, buy, and operate Seller Systems
Guide architecture and investment decisions, weighing trade-offs between build vs. buy, modernization vs. extension, and platform vs. point solution
Interact with external users and propose critical infrastructure abstractions that best serve them
Validate product ideas and prototypes with early adopters
Define key success metrics for developer products
Work on infrastructure, API, and data models and pipelines roadmaps
Work on internal AI agents for specific functions such as Recruiting, Legal, etc. to aid productivity and great customer experience

green_flags: Clearly defined team (IDX) and product scope across multiple engineering pillars
Partner with CTO signals high organizational visibility and influence
JD explicitly states preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement — inclusive application language
Stripe's scale and mission provide clear product impact context
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated — unclear where the role is based or what flexibility exists
No visa sponsorship or work authorization information provided
Combination of hard requirements across AI/ML, infrastructure, enterprise PM, and LLMs creates a very narrow candidate profile
AI and Technical Expertise section is listed separately from minimum requirements but uses language equally as mandatory — ambiguous whether these are truly required or preferred

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and partner-with-CTO scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated explicitly)
location.type (not stated in JD body; left as 'unclear')
ambiguities: The 'AI and Technical Expertise' section appears between 'Minimum requirements' and 'Preferred qualifications' sections but is not explicitly labeled as either. It is treated here as hard/must-have based on its structural position and unconditional language ('Hands-on experience', 'Prior experience', 'Robust functional and technical understanding').
Location and remote eligibility are entirely absent from the JD — location.type left as 'unclear'.
No visa sponsorship information is present in the JD.
